paper.title: LP 543-25: a rare low-mass runaway disk star

extraction.summary: No Galactic-unbound HVS candidates. Paper studies a single low-mass star LP 543-25 (Gaia DR2 3137866242753594624) using Gaia DR2 astrometry. The paper explicitly states the star is bound to the Galaxy (line 56: 'i.e. it is bound to the Galaxy'). The star has Galactocentric velocity components U=-195, V=-34, W=37 km/s, moving in the Galactic plane at ~200 km/s away from the Galactic Center. Classified as a low-mass runaway disk star, not a hypervelocity or unbound star.

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 astrometry (RA, Dec, parallax, proper motions) for LP 543-25 (source 3137866242753594624). No radial velocity in Gaia DR2 for this star.
outputs: Gaia DR2 astrometric parameters for LP 543-25

source_refs.context: Gaia DR2 provides RA, Dec, parallax, proper motions. Source ID 3137866242753594624.

method_chain.summary: Compute heliocentric and Galactocentric Galactic velocity components following Johnson & Soderblom (1987). Assumes negligible radial velocity. Solar motion from Schönrich et al. (2010).

source_refs.context: Galactic velocity computation and result: U=206, V=-289, W=30 km/s.

method_chain.summary: Paper states star is bound to Galaxy. Compares with known low-mass runaway stars. Concludes LP 543-25 is a low-mass runaway disk star.
outputs: Bound status: bound to Galaxy. Classification: low-mass runaway disk star.

source_refs.context: 'i.e. it is bound to the Galaxy' and comparison with other low-mass runaways.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Single star LP 543-25 (PSS 544-7, Gaia DR2 3137866242753594624). High proper-motion star at 458 pc, previously proposed as a candidate cannonball star ejected by a star cluster. Revisited with Gaia DR2.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Paper explicitly concludes LP 543-25 is bound to the Galaxy ('i.e. it is bound to the Galaxy', line 56). Galactocentric velocity ~200 km/s in the Galactic plane is typical of a runaway star but not an unbound HVS. Classified as a low-mass runaway disk star.

source_refs.context: Full paper text: introduction, Gaia DR2 kinematics, bound conclusion, runaway classification.
